question: The aim is to design an automatic storage and retrieval system that must have a storage capacity of 120 products, each product occupying 1 compartment. Due to the space available at ground level, the storage system can have 2 aisles and 10 products can be stored horizontally on each side of each aisle. How many (vertical) levels should the automatic storage and retrieval system have in order to have the above mentioned storage capacity? Calculations:

Each aisle of a three-aisle AS/RS contains 4 storage compartments in the length direction and 4 compartments vertically. All storage compartments will be the same size to accommodate standard size pallets of dimensions x=1 m, y=1 m. The height of a unit load is z=1 m and the allowances are a=0.1 m, b=0.1 m and c=0.1 m. What is the width of the AS/RS? Calculations:
For a 10-digit coded product using the polycode structure, what is the number of levels of the tree required to represent the product characteristics?

options.A: additional information is needed to determine the number of levels
options.B: the product characteristics are not represented as a tree
options.C: the number of digits minus 2, that is 8
options.D: the number of digits, that is 10

question: Group technology is not used if:

options.A: the products can be grouped into product families
options.C: the products can't be grouped into product cells

options.A: the groups on the main diagonal indicate the storage cells
options.B: each group on the main diagonal indicates the group of machines used for a group of parts
options.C: each group of the matrix indicates the distance between two machines belonging to the same manufacturing cell
options.D: each group of the matrix indicates the distance between the manufacturing cells

question: To be flexible a manufacturing system must:

options.B: quickly adapt to a new product
options.C: be able to identify components and processed products
options.D: be able to differentiate the components and products processed
options.E: be controlled by a hierarchical control system

question: Just-in-time production system is based on:
The objective(s) of Lean manufacturing is/are:
The Master Production Schedule (MPS) is generally developed for the long term.
Which of the following associations is/are correct?

options.A: hierarchical control system <=> traditional manufacturing system
options.B: distributed control system <=> traditional manufacturing system
options.C: hierarchical control system <=> modern manufacturing system
options.D: distributed control system <=> modern manufacturing system

question:
What is the right order of the general steps required for the management activities of an investment project?
a. the equipment is installed and debugged.
b. vendor quotations are required.
c. any special tooling and supplies are ordered.
d. the proposal to justify the investment is prepared.
e. management approvals are granted for the investment.
f. operators are trained.
g. order is placed to the winning vendor.
h. responsibility for running the equipment is turned over to the operating department.

options.A: d, b, e, g, c, f, a, h
options.B: a, d, b, e, g, c, f, h
options.C: e, b, d, g, c, f, a, h
options.D: d, b, e, g, c, a, f, h

options.A: capability to adapt a given machine in the system to a wide range of production operations and part styles
options.B: the range of part styles that can be produced on the system
options.C: ability to change the product mix while maintaining the same total production quantity
options.D: ease with which design changes can be accommodated and new product can be introduced
options.E: capability to produce parts through alternative workstation sequences in response to breakdowns, tool failures, and other interruptions at individual stations
options.F: ability to economically produce parts in high and low quantities of production, given the fixed investment in the system
